_comment: P37 Florida jurisdictional overlays (optional bucket, advisory only). attribute + versioned-fact records carry fully-cited real facts. geometry records carry a geometry_ref to the authoritative endpoint plus a clearly-labeled ILLUSTRATIVE bbox only (a real boundary is resolved via the live connector, jurisdictional_overlay_live, or user-supplied GeoJSON) -- no fabricated boundaries. Never a legal or permitting determination.
title: High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) - Miami-Dade and Broward
text: The Florida Building Code High-Velocity Hurricane Zone applies to all of Miami-Dade and Broward counties by definition, so it is an attribute test on county FIPS (12086 Miami-Dade, 12011 Broward), not a boundary polygon. In the HVHZ, exterior products must be impact-rated and carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) / TAS approval. Advisory only; confirm the current FBC edition and product requirements with the authority having jurisdiction.

title: SB 4D milestone structural inspection (condo/co-op, 3+ stories)
text: Florida Statute 553.899 requires a milestone structural inspection for condominium and cooperative buildings three or more habitable stories tall: the initial inspection by December 31 of the year the building turns 30 (from the certificate of occupancy date), then every 10 years; a local enforcement agency may lower the trigger to 25 years for coastal exposure. Attribute test on ownership form + stories + building age. Advisory only; the local building official is authoritative.

title: Sea-level-rise projection for resilience freeboard (SE FL Compact)
text: The Southeast Florida Regional Climate Compact Unified Sea Level Rise Projection informs additional resilience freeboard set by local floodplain ordinances (Broward, Miami-Dade, Palm Beach, Monroe). This is a versioned fact tied to its projection edition (2019 current), not a boundary; the specific freeboard value is set by the adopted local ordinance and is null here until supplied per deployment (no-fabrication).

title: FEMA flood hazard zone (NFHL)
text: Whether a project point falls in a FEMA Special Flood Hazard Area (e.g. zone AE, VE) and its base flood elevation. Resolved by a live point query to the FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer (layer 28) when the live connector is enabled, or from a user-supplied determination. Advisory only; not a substitute for an official FEMA flood determination.

title: Local historic-preservation district (design review)
text: Whether a project point falls inside a designated local historic-preservation district that triggers design review (e.g. matching historic frame profiles). The authoritative boundary is the municipal ArcGIS historic-districts layer; resolve it via the live connector or a user-supplied GeoJSON. The bbox below is an ILLUSTRATIVE placeholder only, not the real district boundary. Advisory only.

title: Historic-district frame-profile requirement (conflict example alpha)
text: Within a designated historic district, exterior openings must preserve the original historic frame/mullion profile. This can collide with the HVHZ impact-window requirement (fl-hvhz), a genuine legal conflict (structural safety vs historic aesthetics) the engine escalates to human review rather than auto-resolving.
